Since User must buy at least one of each item, we have \( x \geq 1 \), \( y \geq 1 \), and \( z \geq 1 \). We want to maximize \( z \). First, we note that \( x + 2y \) must be at least 3 (since \( x \geq 1 \) and \( y \geq 1 \)). Therefore, the maximum value of \( z \) is when \( x + 2y \) is minimized, i.e., 3. So, \( 7z = 50 - 3 = 47 \). Since 47 is not divisible by 7, we try the next smallest value for \( x + 2y \), which is 5. So, \( 7z = 50 - 5 = 45 \), and \( z = 45 / 7 = 6.4286 \). Since \( z \) must be an integer, we get \( z = 6 \). Now we need to check if there are values of \( x \) and \( y \) that satisfy \( x + 2y = 5 \) and \( x \geq 1 \) and \( y \geq 1 \). If \( y = 1 \), then \( x = 3 \), which is valid. Therefore, the largest number of jogs User can purchase is 6. </think> <answer> 6 </answer>
